channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: 'House of the Dragon,' Season 3, Episode 5: A not-so-secret door is just a door
item.description: We finally find Aemond. Meanwhile, Alicent and Helaena are doing a little too well trying not to be found.
item.content:encoded:
We finally find Aemond. Meanwhile, Alicent and Helaena are doing a little too well trying not to be found.

(Image credit: Ollie Upton)
item.dc:creator: Glen Weldon

item.title: Romance books recommended by NPR staff for summer reading
item.description: Summer is great for some sizzling reads and NPR staff have recommendations for romance novels from our Books We Love list.
item.content:encoded: Summer is great for some sizzling reads and NPR staff have recommendations for romance novels from our Books We Love list.
item.dc:creator: Wailin Wong

item.title: A cavalcade of international stars headlined the World Cup's first-ever halftime final show
item.description: With President Trump in attendance, an all-superstar lineup that included Madonna, BTS, Justin Bieber and Burna Boy felt a little more political than it might have otherwise.
item.content:encoded:
With President Trump in attendance, an all-superstar lineup that included Madonna, BTS, Justin Bieber and Burna Boy felt a little more political than it might have otherwise.

(Image credit: Mauro Pimentel)
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Tsioulcas

item.title: Old-school digicams are everywhere. What's behind the return of retro tech?
item.description: People who weren't yet alive in the 1990s and early 2000s are buying up old-school point-and-shoot cameras. For some, it's a trendy retro vibe; for other, a rebellion against the smartphone era.
item.content:encoded: People who weren't yet alive in the 1990s and early 2000s are buying up old-school point-and-shoot cameras. For some, it's a trendy retro vibe; for other, a rebellion against the smartphone era.
item.dc:creator: Alina Selyukh

item.title: Sunday Puzzle: A test of WI-T
item.description: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe plays the puzzle along with Weekend Edition Puzzlemaster Will Shortz and WFDD listener John Dudley of Winston Salem, N.C.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe plays the puzzle along with Weekend Edition Puzzlemaster Will Shortz and WFDD listener John Dudley of Winston Salem, N.C.
item.dc:creator: Will Shortz

item.title: 5 clever design tricks to make a small home look bigger
item.description: Just because you live in a small place doesn't mean it has to look or feel cramped. Interior designers share creative strategies to create the illusion of space that work for renters and small budgets.
item.content:encoded:
Just because you live in a small place doesn't mean it has to look or feel cramped. Interior designers share creative strategies to create the illusion of space that work for renters and small budgets.

(Image credit: Andee Tagle/NPR)
item.dc:creator: Andee Tagle

item.title: Cuban artist and dissident exiled from country after 5 years in prison arrives in US
item.description: A famous Cuban dissident artist and musician, Luis Manuel Otero Alcántara, arrived in Miami on Saturday after being released from a five-year prison sentence on the condition that he leave his country.
item.content:encoded:
A famous Cuban dissident artist and musician, Luis Manuel Otero Alcántara, arrived in Miami on Saturday after being released from a five-year prison sentence on the condition that he leave his country.

(Image credit: David Santiago)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Wait Wait for July 18. 2026: With Not My Job guest Vicki Peterson
item.description: This week, Wait Wait is live in Chicago with guest host Tom Papa, special guest Vicki Peterson and panelists Alonzo Bodden, Helen Hong, and Dulcé Sloan
item.content:encoded:
This week, Wait Wait is live in Chicago with guest host Tom Papa, special guest Vicki Peterson and panelists Alonzo Bodden, Helen Hong, and Dulcé Sloan

(Image credit: Chelsea Guglielmino)

item.title: The 2000s called. They want their digital camera back
item.description: Why are people who weren't born 25 years ago snapping up the digital camera of that era? Blame Taylor Swift, trend cycles, childhood nostalgia and smartphone fatigue.
item.content:encoded: Why are people who weren't born 25 years ago snapping up the digital camera of that era? Blame Taylor Swift, trend cycles, childhood nostalgia and smartphone fatigue.
item.dc:creator: Alina Selyukh

item.title: This unusual, Johnny Carson-backed fund has helped Nebraska arts survive — until now
item.description: A unique public-private endowment has long shielded Nebraska's cultural scene from federal budget cuts. But a massive state deficit is putting its future in jeopardy.
item.content:encoded:
A unique public-private endowment has long shielded Nebraska's cultural scene from federal budget cuts. But a massive state deficit is putting its future in jeopardy.

(Image credit: Walker Pickering for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman
